rationale: Plan decomposition is sound. Phase 1 (SETTLE reorder, confidence 0.95) is the critical path and should execute first. Phase 2 (unit check, confidence 0.60) is secondary safeguard. Phase 3 (MTT gate relax) is low-risk and should run in parallel with Phase 1 (zero coupling). Gates 1.5 and 4.3 are sufficient to catch NPT divergence.
key_decisions: Phase 1 before Phase 2: SETTLE-thermostat mismatch (0.95 confidence) is the smoking gun; GROMACS comparison validates. Unit conversion (0.60) is secondary.
Phase 3 (MTT) runs in parallel with Phase 1: no code dependencies, ~30min fix, theory-backed.
Gates 1.5 and 4.3 are sufficient for stability validation. Recommend adding pressure-drift and equipartition assertions to 1.5 (non-blocking enhancement).
Escalation: Single retry of Phase 1.5 with KE logging on first failure, then trigger Phase 2. If Phase 2 finds nothing and 1.5 still fails, halt.
parallelizable: Phase 3 (MTT gate fix) runs during Phase 1 execution
ship_readiness: Phase 4 pass → ship confidence 0.80 for NPT v1.0 (conditional on 100ps post-merge spot-check)
next_action: Dispatch Fixer to execute Phase 1 (SETTLE reorder). Dispatch General-purpose to execute Phase 3 (MTT gate relax) in parallel.
